reasoning: The EUR/USD pair requires a drop below 1.0000 to resolve YES. Since 2024, it has approached this level, bottoming at 1.0350 in late December 2024 and 1.0257 in early January 2025. Although numerous late-2024 macro forecasts from major banks predicted parity by late 2025 due to potential U.S. tariffs and an aggressive ECB easing cycle relative to the Fed, recent March 2025 data shows a bullish reversal for the Euro. Weakening U.S. data has prompted expectations of near-term Federal Reserve rate cuts, pushing the pair back towards the $1.08-$1.10 range. The requirement is merely to *touch* <1.0000 at any point before 2026, which historically has roughly double the probability of *closing* below a barrier, but the recent upswing means an ~8-10% decline is now needed within ~9.5 months. Given the strong psychological support at parity and the recent reversal reducing momentum, I am adjusting downwards slightly from the early March community prediction of 35% to 28%.
